screenshots.status: SUCCESS — page loads cleanly. Vibe at capture: 🔥 Crushing It. Night owl mode active (Monday, Mar 9 late night). All 5 workers at desks, VACANT slot present, MIDNIGHT window visible top-right of office floor.

score_note: Accessibility dropped from 3→2.5 (new issues compounding unfixed ones). Code quality dropped from 2.5→2 (four reviews of the same five unfixed items — this is now a process failure, not just tech debt). Fun and features holding strong. The app is delightful. The codebase is getting harder to maintain.

carry_over_resolved.office_jsx_split: NOT FIXED ❌ [4 REVIEWS] — still exactly 1515 lines. This is now escalated to sprint blocker. Four consecutive reviews with zero progress.
carry_over_resolved.fragmented_media_queries: NOT FIXED ❌ [4 REVIEWS] — Office.css now has TWELVE @media blocks (11 prefers-reduced-motion + 3 max-width:480px + 1 hover:none at line 608 + 1 hover:none at 2382 + 1 max-width:600px + 1 max-width:768px). App.css has 7 prefers-reduced-motion blocks. Total: 18+ prefers-reduced-motion fragments that should be 2.
carry_over_resolved.lone_survivor_label_inline_styles: NOT FIXED ❌ [4 REVIEWS] — Office.jsx line 1419 still has a 7-property inline style object on .lone-survivor-label. This is a 10-minute fix. Four reviews of asking.
carry_over_resolved.vibe_weather_constants_in_utils: NOT FIXED ❌ [4 REVIEWS] — VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS are still module-level constants in Office.jsx (lines 108–121).
carry_over_resolved.inner_setTimeout_memory_leak: NOT FIXED ❌ [3 REVIEWS] — Office.jsx idle bubble effect (lines 489, 493) and error bubble effect (lines 504, 510) still have inner setTimeout calls not tracked or cancelled in cleanup. If a Character unmounts mid-animation, the inner timer fires setState on an unmounted component.
carry_over_resolved.speech_bubble_clipped: NOT FIXED ❌ [3 REVIEWS] — Desktop screenshot confirms it: Bloberto's speech bubble is still guillotined at the top of the office-floor. We can see '...hour?! Legends. 🏆' at the very top edge — the bubble is rendered outside the overflow:hidden boundary. Office.jsx line 1401: top={4} is too close to the container ceiling for bottom: calc(100% + 8px) to fit within the parent's overflow:hidden.

visual_observations.desktop: Dark theme solid. All five workers rendered with correct role-colored avatars. Bloberto at CVO desk, 🏆 trophy visible (Crushing It vibe). SOFIA name label in red — she's the active/highlighted worker. Conference table (lower-left) with orbital dots animated. MIDNIGHT window visible (top-right, correct for late-night). Whiteboard shows 'SHIP IT' with avg progress bar. Ficus (orange, right side), cactus, small potted plant scattered. WallClock SVG visible at top-left of office floor wall (new). Shortcut toast present at bottom-right. Speech bubble still clipped at the top — 'hour?! Legends.' visible as a stub.
visual_observations.mobile: Layout holds reasonably. All characters legible. VACANT label visible. MIDNIGHT monitor present. Conference table at bottom-left. Footer footer text readable. Office floor scales to fit 375px width. Characters are small but distinguishable. The left edge of Sofia's desk area shows her red avatar glow at the boundary of the container.

suggestions.title: [4 REVIEWS] Office.jsx 1515 lines — escalating to sprint blocker
suggestions.description: Office.jsx has not shrunk by a single line across FOUR consecutive QA reviews. It contains at minimum five separable concerns: (1) SVG screen data DESK_SCREEN_SVG (~100 lines), (2) all CharacterAvatar SVG variants (~370 lines), (3) ambient UI components: WallClock, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, AwaySign, VibeShockwave (~340 lines), (4) module-level constants: BLOBERTO_TITLES, VIBE_WEATHER_*, LONE_SURVIVOR_LABELS, VACANT_ADS, PING_REACTIONS, VIBE_COLORS, VIBE_RANK, VIBE_SHOCKWAVE_COLORS, CLOCK_COMMENTS (~100 lines), (5) the main Office component (~600 lines). Proposed split: src/components/CharacterAvatar.jsx, src/components/OfficeAmbience.jsx, src/data/officeConstants.js. Assign a dedicated sprint story. No new features in Office.jsx until this is split.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Inner setTimeouts in idle/error bubble effects not cancelled on unmount
suggestions.description: Office.jsx idle effect (lines ~488–498): `setInterval(() => { setIdleBubble(true); setTimeout(() => setIdleBubble(false), 2200) }, ...)` and `setTimeout(() => { setIdleBubble(true); setTimeout(() => setIdleBubble(false), 2200) }, ...)`. The cleanup only calls clearInterval(interval) and clearTimeout(initialTimeout). The two INNER setTimeout calls — the ones that set state back to false — are never tracked or cancelled. Same pattern in the error bubble effect (lines ~503–513). If a Character unmounts while an inner timer is in flight (e.g. worker goes offline during animation), the timer fires and calls setState on an unmounted component. Fix: collect inner timer IDs in a ref array (innerTimerRefs.current.push(...)) and clear them all in the cleanup. This is a two-line fix per effect.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Bloberto speech bubble clipped by office-floor overflow:hidden
suggestions.description: Still visible in qa34-desktop.png: speech bubble text 'hour?! Legends. 🏆' appears as a partial strip at the top of the office-floor container. Office.jsx line 1401: Bloberto is placed at top={4}%, and .speech-bubble is positioned `bottom: calc(100% + 8px)` above the character. At 4% from the top, the bubble extends above the container and is cut by Office.css line 84: `overflow: hidden` on .office-floor. Three fixes available: (a) increase Bloberto's `top` prop to ~14% and adjust mgr-desk top in CSS to match, (b) use `overflow: visible` on a wrapper scoped to the manager area, or (c) use `clip-path` instead of `overflow: hidden` on .office-floor so absolutely-positioned children can escape. Option (a) is simplest.

suggestions.title: Tab title cycling creates leaked interval when tab becomes visible with active initial timer
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 978–1006: the tab title effect uses `let timer`. When `handleVisibilityChange` fires with `visibilityState === 'visible'`, it executes `timer = setInterval(B, 8000)` — but only AFTER having done `clearInterval(timer)` in the hidden branch. The problem: the hidden branch clears the OLD interval and sets static title. The visible branch creates NEW interval B and assigns it to `timer`. So far so good. BUT: if the page is freshly loaded and the initial `timer = setInterval(A)` is running, and `visibilitychange` somehow fires `visible` (e.g., page restored from bfcache, focus event misfire, or OS-level tab restore), `handleVisibilityChange` creates B without clearing A first (A was never in the hidden path). `timer` is now B, cleanup clears B. A runs forever until the effect deps change again and both get GC'd. Additionally, the hidden→visible→hidden→visible rapid-fire scenario: visible branch creates B without a guard that checks if a timer is already running. Fix: at the start of the visible branch in `handleVisibilityChange`, add `clearInterval(timer)` before assigning the new interval, mirroring the hidden branch. Better still: convert to `timerRef.current` as Sofia has asked since review #33.

suggestions.title: [4 REVIEWS] Fragmented @media blocks — consolidate NOW
suggestions.description: Office.css: 11 `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` blocks (lines 2, 714, 1068, 1454, 1577, 1614, 1654, 1691, 1701, 1996, 2033), 3 `@media (max-width: 480px)` blocks (lines 1133, 2165, 2316), plus new additions at 608 and 2382 for `@media (hover: none)`. App.css: 7 `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` blocks. Total: 18 motion-preference fragments that should be one per file. This makes accessibility auditing nearly impossible — any new `animation:` or `transition:` added will almost certainly not get a corresponding reduced-motion override because no one knows which block to add it to. Tooling suggestion: add a stylelint rule `no-duplicate-at-rules` or use PostCSS groupMediaQueries to enforce consolidation.

suggestions.title: skip-link is not the first focusable element — it's buried after toast elements
suggestions.description:
App.jsx line 1076: `Skip to content` is rendered AFTER `{completionToast &&
...
}` and ``. Per WCAG 2.4.1, a skip navigation link must be the first focusable element a keyboard user encounters. Currently a keyboard user tabbing through the page hits at minimum the ShortcutToast's internal focusable elements before reaching the skip link. Move the skip link to the very top of the return JSX — before ALL conditional renders, portals, and toasts. The skip link should be the literal first child of the root div.

suggestions.title: footer-tagline span is not keyboard accessible — new feature, new oversight
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1300–1307: the footer tagline cycling span (`className='footer-tagline'`) has an `onClick` handler but NO `role='button'`, NO `tabIndex`, and NO `onKeyDown`. Keyboard users and screen reader users cannot discover or activate the tagline cycler. The `style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }}` is purely visual. A proper fix: replace `<span>` with `<button type='button' className='footer-tagline'>` — or keep span but add `role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); ... } }}`. Additionally the inline `style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }}` should become a `.footer-tagline { cursor: pointer; }` CSS rule.

suggestions.title: role='radio' filter buttons have no role='radiogroup' container
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1290: worker filter buttons use `role='radio'` and `aria-checked` but are wrapped only in a generic `<div>`. Per WAI-ARIA spec, `role='radio'` elements MUST be owned by a `role='radiogroup'` (or `role='group'`) element to form a valid widget. Without the container, assistive technologies may not announce these as a radio group, and arrow-key navigation between options won't work as expected. Fix: add `role='radiogroup'` and `aria-label='Filter by worker'` to the wrapper div. Alternatively, use real `<input type='radio'>` elements styled as buttons — which gives UA arrow-key support for free.

suggestions.title: [4 REVIEWS] VIBE_WEATHER constants still in Office.jsx, not in utils
suggestions.description: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS (lines 108–114) and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS (lines 115–121) are still module-level constants in Office.jsx despite utils/constants.js existing. As noted in reviews #31, #32, #33, and now #34. Additionally, CLOCK_COMMENTS (a new object added inside the WallClock component, lines ~975–982) is defined inside the component function body — re-created on every render. It should be a module-level constant (or move to utils). All constants that don't depend on component state or props belong outside component functions.

suggestions.title: [4 REVIEWS] lone-survivor-label still has 7-property inline style
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1419: `<div className='lone-survivor-label' style={{ fontSize: '0.45rem', opacity: 0.6, position: 'absolute', bottom: '-0.6rem', left: '50%', transform: 'translateX(-50%)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.7)' }}>`. All seven properties should live in a `.lone-survivor-label` CSS rule in Office.css. This was asked in reviews #31, #32, #33, and #34. It is a literally 4-minute fix.

suggestions.title: New inline styles added this cycle: WallClock positioning, character cursor, footer counter
suggestions.description: Three new inline style instances were added since review #33: (1) Office.jsx line 980: WallClock SVG `style={{ position: 'absolute', left: '22%', top: '2.5%', zIndex: 1, pointerEvents: 'auto' }}` — the `.office-wall-clock` class already exists in Office.css; positioning should be defined there. (2) Office.jsx line 640: `style={{ ...style, ...(handleClick ? { cursor: 'pointer' } : {}) }}` — add `.char--clickable { cursor: pointer; }` class. (3) App.jsx line 1305: `<span style={{ opacity: 0.55, fontSize: '0.7em', fontVariantNumeric: 'tabular-nums' }}>` for the quote counter — add `.footer-quote-counter` class. The inline style count is now 34 in Office.jsx and 25 in App.jsx and trending upward. Every new feature seems to add more.

suggestions.title: [2 REVIEWS] WorkerCard uses role='article' — semantically incorrect
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 261: `role='article'` on each WorkerCard div. WAI-ARIA defines 'article' as an independent document-like composition. Each WorkerCard is an interactive widget, not an article. Better: use `role='group'` with `aria-labelledby` pointing to the worker name heading. Wrap the worker grid in a `role='list'` container and use `role='listitem'` per card for the simplest correct semantics. The current `role='article'` causes screen readers to announce each card as a full document, which is disorienting.

suggestions.title: [2 REVIEWS] stuckWorkers[0].emoji access — undefined for most workers
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 477: `stuckWorkers[0].emoji` — the `.emoji` field is only defined on workers that explicitly have it (only `luna` in DEFAULT_ROSTER has `emoji: '🌙'`). For all other workers, this renders `undefined ${name}` in the stuck worker stat card. Fall back to `getRoleEmoji(stuckWorkers[0].role)` from utils, which already exists and covers all roles via ROLE_EMOJIS.

suggestions.title: plantToast and completionToast have near-identical 12-property inline style objects
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1166–1189 (plantToast) and App.jsx lines 1052–1073 (completionToast) both render with nearly identical inline style objects: position:fixed, bottom:2rem, left:50%, transform:translateX(-50%), background:var(--surface2), borderRadius:12px, padding:0.6rem 1.2rem, fontSize:0.95rem, fontWeight:600, zIndex:9999, whiteSpace:nowrap, pointerEvents:none, animation:badge-pop. Only the `border` color differs. Create `.toast-popup` in App.css with all shared properties. Use `--toast-border-color` CSS variable for the dynamic border. This eliminates ~20 lines of inline JSX style and makes both toasts consistent.

suggestions.title: [2 REVIEWS] Ficus streak tooltip inaccessible — still only title attribute
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1452–1462: the ficus element remains `aria-hidden='true'` with streak info only in `title` attribute. Mouse-hover only. Add a `<span className='sr-only'>` inside the ficus (even if aria-hidden on the image parent, a sr-only sibling outside the aria-hidden element can still be read) that announces the streak state. Or better: the vibe streak counter should appear as visually hidden text adjacent to the ficus, outside the aria-hidden boundary.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast mounts and runs timers on coarse-pointer devices it immediately hides
suggestions.description: App.jsx: ShortcutToast still mounts on touch devices, runs through 2 timers (1500ms show, 7000ms auto-dismiss), writes localStorage, then gets hidden by `@media (pointer: coarse) { .shortcut-toast { display: none !important; } }`. All this work to be hidden. Add a guard at the start of ShortcutToast: `if (window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches) return null;` — skip the entire component on touch devices. Alternatively initialize `phase` as `'hidden'` on coarse-pointer devices to skip the timer chain.

suggestions.title: DESK_SCREEN_SVG is 98 lines of JSX at module level in Office.jsx
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 8–98: DESK_SCREEN_SVG is a static object containing JSX SVG elements. Logically correct (never mutates), but contributes significantly to the 1515-line file. When Office.jsx is finally split, DESK_SCREEN_SVG should move to src/data/deskScreens.jsx or be co-located with the desk component.

suggestions.title: WallClock has charming CLOCK_COMMENTS but they're unreachable — `title` only shows on hover, never on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WallClock component (lines ~975–990): CLOCK_COMMENTS is a lovely object with per-vibe quips ('Time has no meaning during an incident.', 'You have checked this three times in the last hour.'). However, these are surfaced only via the SVG `<title>` element — which is shown as a tooltip on desktop hover only, and is invisible on mobile. On mobile you can see the clock icon but will never encounter the commentary. Low priority but: consider making the clock clickable to show a brief toast with the comment, or add it to the WallClock's aria-label. Currently a fun feature that ~50% of users never see.

suggestions.title: CLOCK_COMMENTS defined inside WallClock function — re-created every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: `const CLOCK_COMMENTS = { crushing: '...', ... }` is defined inside the WallClock component body (lines ~975–982). This object is re-created on every render of WallClock. Since WallClock re-renders every 60 seconds (its setInterval), it's not a hot path — but the principle of static data living outside component functions is consistent and important. Move to module scope alongside the other constants.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard wb-doodle ASCII art truncates on narrow containers
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1237: `<pre className='wb-doodle'>` renders ASCII art like `(╯°□°)╯︵ ┻━┻` which is ~14 characters wide. At mobile scale the whiteboard element is quite small and this text may overflow or wrap awkwardly. Confirm at 375px viewport width. Add `overflow: hidden; text-overflow: clip; white-space: nowrap; font-size: clamp(0.3rem, 1.2vw, 0.5rem)` to `.wb-doodle` in Office.css if not already handled.

whats_good: WallClock component is a new gem — per-vibe commentary in CLOCK_COMMENTS shows real craft ('Still here? So am I. I am a clock. That is fine for me.' 😭).
Footer tagline cycler with sparkle animation (✦) is a lovely easter egg — the quoteBonus mechanic with show/hide toggle and localStorage memory is delightful.
MIDNIGHT window rendering (hour < 5 → midnight sky with stars) adds ambient atmosphere for late-night viewers. Nice touch for the current review session.
Whiteboard wb-doodle ASCII art is charming and vibe-reactive: `(╯°□°)╯︵ ┻━┻` on-fire, `╰(*°▽°*)╯` crushing. Chef's kiss.
Vibe history dots on the whiteboard (wb-history-dots) — subtle but great context for where the team has been.
WallClock cleans up its setInterval correctly — simple, correct, no leaks.
Character component correctly handles keyboard Enter/Space for clickable mode (line 645: onKeyDown handler present).
onFocus/onBlur mirroring onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave on characters — keyboard users get speech bubble tooltips too.
No console errors on load. No network failures. API data hydrates cleanly.
Conference table animation dots with meetingWorkers/standupWorkers logic is a subtle UX delight.
The footer quote counter display (`2/8`) gives satisfying completionist progress feedback.
process_note: Sofia's concern, on the record: five items have now been flagged across four reviews. The inner setTimeout leak has been flagged three times. The speech bubble clip has been flagged three times. None of them are hard. Carlos and Maya: are these items in your backlog or are they falling off? If they're known and deprioritized, fine — say so and I'll stop flagging them. If they're unknown, we have a backlog hygiene problem. Either way, by review #35 I want a documented decision on each item: fix date, or explicit 'won't fix' with rationale.
